prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='CWT_catboost', seasonality_champion='ETS', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: Analysis reveals extreme model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=1.62) and very high seasonality variance (0.96), indicating models capture patterns fundamentally differently. FT_svr dominates overall RMSE while ETS/THETA excel at seasonality, with CWT_catboost leading trend tracking. The combination of strong early-horizon specialists (FT_svr) and late-horizon specialists (ONLY_DWT_catboost) suggests horizon-specific weighting would help, but the extreme disagreement warrants DBA combination to leverage diverse model behaviors without overfitting to any single pattern.
prompts.skeptic:
Chame build_debate_packet_tool() PRIMEIRO (inputs via context). Depois retorne APENAS JSON (sem markdown) com add_names, remove_names, params_overrides, rationale, changes, when_good.
IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "CWT_catboost",
  "seasonality_champion": "ETS",
  "overall_champion": "FT_svr",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "FT_svr", "late": "ONLY_DWT_catboost"},
  "tier1_models": ["FT_svr", "ONLY_FT_rf", "ONLY_DWT_rf", "ONLY_CWT_rf", "THETA", "ETS", "ONLY_DWT_catboost", "FT_catboost"],
  "tier2_models": ["ARIMA", "CWT_catboost", "ONLY_CWT_catboost", "ONLY_FT_svr", "NaiveMovingAverage", "FT_rf", "DWT_catboost", "ONLY_FT_catboost"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 1.6171,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["up", "up", "up"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "Analysis reveals extreme model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=1.62) and very high seasonality variance (0.96), indicating models capture patterns fundamentally differently. FT_svr dominates overall RMSE while ETS/THETA excel at seasonality, with CWT_catboost leading trend tracking. The combination of strong early-horizon specialists (FT_svr) and late-horizon specialists (ONLY_DWT_catboost) suggests horizon-specific weighting would help, but the extreme disagreement warrants DBA combination to leverage diverse model behaviors without overfitting to any single pattern."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k3", "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35", "best_per_horizon_by_validation"],
  "params_overrides": {"topk_mean_per_horizon_k3": {"top_k": 3}, "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35": {"top_k": 3, "shrinkage": 0.35}},
  "score_preset": "rmse_focus",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=9 indicates strong horizon heterogeneity favoring per-horizon selection. relative_spread_mean=0.3317 (≥0.25) and rmse_spread_ratio=1.62 warrant DBA combination for phase-shift robustness. FT_svr dominates with RMSE=37531 vs DWT_svr at 106941 (184% gap), justifying inverse_rmse_weights with shrinkage. n_windows=3 requires small top_k=3 and shrinkage≥0.3 to avoid overfitting. Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination for high seasonality variance (0.96)."
}
raw.skeptic:
{
  "add_names": [],
  "remove_names": [],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "rationale": "Current set has 4 candidates with good diversity: 1 baseline (dba_combination), 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3), 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35). All types are represented. n_windows=3 from validation_summary is sufficient for weighted strategies with anti-leakage rolling/expanding selection. No leakage detected - baseline never leaks, and weighted/selection candidates use past windows only per eval_config. Score margin top2 is 0.00087 which is acceptable. Universe leaderboards show topk_mean_per_horizon_k15 (RMSE:49697) beats current best (topk_mean_per_horizon_k3 RMSE:50875), but k3 already in set. No changes needed - candidate set is diverse and well-balanced.",
  "changes": [],
  "when_good": ""
}
raw.statistician: {"add_names": ["topk_mean_per_horizon_k15", "inverse_rmse_weights_k15_sh0.70"], "remove_names": [], "params_overrides": {}, "rationale": "n_windows=3 (low) => recommended_knobs suggest stronger regularization. relative_spread_mean=0.33≥0.25 => high disagreement favors weighted methods. Universe shows topk_mean_per_horizon_k15 (RMSE=49697) and inverse_rmse_weights_k15_sh0.70 (RMSE=49861) beat current best (RMSE=50875). Adding k15 variants aligns with recommended_knobs top_k=5 guidance for few_windows scenarios.", "changes": ["added topk_mean_per_horizon_k15 for better RMSE from universe leaderboard", "added inverse_rmse_weights_k15_sh0.70 for high disagreement scenario"], "when_good": "current set already has diversity (selection + weighted methods), no baselines needed"}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: Analysis reveals extreme model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=1.62) and very high seasonality variance (0.96), indicating models capture patterns fundamentally differently. FT_svr dominates overall RMSE while ETS/THETA excel at seasonality, with CWT_catboost leading trend tracking. The combination of strong early-horizon specialists (FT_svr) and late-horizon specialists (ONLY_DWT_catboost) suggests horizon-specific weighting would help, but the extreme disagreement warrants DBA combination to leverage diverse model behaviors without overfitting to any single pattern.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=9 indicates strong horizon heterogeneity favoring per-horizon selection. relative_spread_mean=0.3317 (≥0.25) and rmse_spread_ratio=1.62 warrant DBA combination for phase-shift robustness. FT_svr dominates with RMSE=37531 vs DWT_svr at 106941 (184% gap), justifying inverse_rmse_weights with shrinkage. n_windows=3 requires small top_k=3 and shrinkage≥0.3 to avoid overfitting. Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination for high seasonality variance (0.96).

skeptic.rationale: Current set has 4 candidates with good diversity: 1 baseline (dba_combination), 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3), 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35). All types are represented. n_windows=3 from validation_summary is sufficient for weighted strategies with anti-leakage rolling/expanding selection. No leakage detected - baseline never leaks, and weighted/selection candidates use past windows only per eval_config. Score margin top2 is 0.00087 which is acceptable. Universe leaderboards show topk_mean_per_horizon_k15 (RMSE:49697) beats current best (topk_mean_per_horizon_k3 RMSE:50875), but k3 already in set. No changes needed - candidate set is diverse and well-balanced.

statistician.rationale: n_windows=3 (low) => recommended_knobs suggest stronger regularization. relative_spread_mean=0.33≥0.25 => high disagreement favors weighted methods. Universe shows topk_mean_per_horizon_k15 (RMSE=49697) and inverse_rmse_weights_k15_sh0.70 (RMSE=49861) beat current best (RMSE=50875). Adding k15 variants aligns with recommended_knobs top_k=5 guidance for few_windows scenarios.
changes: added topk_mean_per_horizon_k15 for better RMSE from universe leaderboard
added inverse_rmse_weights_k15_sh0.70 for high disagreement scenario
statistician.when_good: current set already has diversity (selection + weighted methods), no baselines needed
